title: PillCam-SPECTRA Optical-Property Recovery (analytical core)
source_data_quality_note: Analytical core for the PillCam-SPECTRA pair (paired with L1-512 PWDR wrapper). Recovers spatial-spectral tissue optical properties (mu_a, mu_s')(r, lambda) at GI mucosal surfaces from multispectral diffuse-reflectance capsule frames. Single-physics analytical Principle; serves as physics_core_principle for the v2 physical_with_discrete_readout wrapper L1-512. Distinct from L1-040 DOT (transmission tomography geometry, cm-scale tissue) by capsule reflectance geometry (mm-scale, single-side acquisition, integrated source-detector). Per pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/CLASSIFICATION_SEGMENTATION_PRINCIPLES.md and PWM_V2_GENESIS_INCLUSION.md (the canonical v2 pilot pair).
related_principles: L1-040 (DOT — transmission diffuse optics in bulk tissue, d_principle ~ 0.40)
L1-512 (PillCam-SPECTRA bleeding-region PWDR wrapper — discrete-readout child)

E.description: PillCam-SPECTRA Optical-Property Recovery: analytical forward model for capsule-endoscopic multispectral diffuse reflectance. Light source at capsule LED illuminates GI mucosa at N wavelengths (typically 5-12 in 400-1000 nm range), CMOS camera captures backscattered light. Diffuse reflectance R(mu_a, mu_s', g, rho) follows the modified Beer-Lambert / spatially-resolved diffusion approximation in turbid tissue, where rho is the source-detector separation (~5 mm in PillCam form factor). Recovery is posed as the spectral-spatial inverse problem that recovers (mu_a, mu_s')(r, lambda) and derived chromophore concentrations (HbO2, Hb, water, lipid, melanin) from N_wavelengths frames. The forward DAG has 6 primitives with one coupling constraint (n_c = 1): the multiplicative coupling between absorption coefficient mu_a and detected intensity through diffuse reflectance fluence — same canonical coupling that qPAT exhibits in its first stage, but without the thermoelastic-acoustic transduction. Difficulty tier delta = 3 (standard) given established diffuse-reflectance theory and well-conditioned multispectral acquisition. Mismatch parameters: capsule_to_mucosa_distance, scattering_anisotropy_g, mucosal_curvature, illumination_uniformity, motion_blur, capsule_orientation_drift. Gaussian thermal noise sets the data-fidelity floor.
E.forward_model: I_back(r, lambda) = I_0(lambda) * R_diffuse(mu_a(r, lambda), mu_s'(r, lambda), g, rho) + n; R_diffuse from spatially-resolved diffusion equation (Farrell 1992) or Monte Carlo radiative transfer (MCML); chromophore decomposition: mu_a(r, lambda) = sum_chrom [c_chrom(r) * epsilon_chrom(lambda)]; jointly invert {I_back(r, lambda)}_lambda -> (c_HbO2, c_Hb, c_water, c_lipid, c_melanin)(r) given source spectrum I_0, capsule geometry rho, scattering anisotropy g
E.world_state_x: Spatial-spectral mucosal optical state: (mu_a(r, lambda), mu_s'(r, lambda)) over the field-of-view of a single PillCam frame (~30-50 mm^2 mucosal patch at 1-3 mm working distance), with chromophore concentrations as the derived clinical quantity. Range: mucosal mu_a 0.1-5 cm^-1; mu_s' 5-30 cm^-1; HbO2 fraction 0.6-0.95.
E.observation_y: Multispectral capsule frame: I_back(x, y, lambda) for N_wavelengths (typically 5-12), single CMOS exposure per band; total frame size 256x256 to 1024x1024 pixels.

G.dag: L.optical_source -> L.diffuse_reflectance -> L.beer_lambert -> L.detector_response -> int.spatial -> int.spectral
coupling_constraints: fluence_to_absorption: detected backscatter intensity is multiplicatively coupled to mu_a through the diffuse-reflectance fluence kernel R_diffuse(mu_a, mu_s', rho) — same multiplicative coupling that qPAT exhibits, but without the subsequent thermoelastic-acoustic transduction

W.regime: Existence of recovered chromophore concentrations is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds under multi-wavelength acquisition with N_wavelengths >= 5 (sufficient to resolve the typical chromophore basis); single-wavelength acquisition is non-unique (cannot decompose mu_a into chromophore components). Stability is well-conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 15) — capsule_to_mucosa_distance dominates absolute-concentration bias; mucosal_curvature contributes a geometric-correction factor; scattering_anisotropy_g uncertainty contributes a scaling factor of order ~10%. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the diffuse-reflectance forward is established by Farrell-Patterson-Wilson 1992 (foundational), Wang-Jacques 1995 (MCML), Yudovsky-Pilon 2010 (multispectral inverse), Bjorgan-Milanic-Randeberg 2014 (hyperspectral classification), and Calin-Parasca-Savastru 2014 (medical hyperspectral).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [least-squares chromophore unmixing; Tikhonov-regularized spatial reconstruction] | analytical [closed-form per-pixel diffuse-reflectance lookup] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [SpectraNet, MicroDiffOptNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||c_hat - c||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_wavelengths * SNR_per_band). Diffuse-reflectance-induced bias adds + C2 * |capsule_to_mucosa_distance| + C3 * |scattering_anisotropy_g| + C4 * |mucosal_curvature| + C5 * ||c - c_s||_1 (best s-sparse approximation residual under chromophore-basis prior). Closed-form per-pixel inversion achieves q=2 (O(1/k^2)) for chromophore unmixing; spatial reconstruction is well-conditioned at the per-pixel level. Deep-prior variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias.
C.complexity: O(H * W * N_wavelengths * N_chromophores) per frame via per-pixel closed-form inversion + spatial smoothing; learned variants O(H W * F_theta_cost)

joint_well_posedness_references: Farrell TJ, Patterson MS, Wilson B (1992) A diffusion theory model of spatially resolved, steady-state diffuse reflectance for the noninvasive determination of tissue optical properties in vivo. Med Phys 19(4):879-888.
Wang LH, Jacques SL, Zheng L (1995) MCML — Monte Carlo modeling of light transport in multi-layered tissues. Comput Methods Programs Biomed 47(2):131-146.
Yudovsky D, Pilon L (2010) Rapid and accurate estimation of blood saturation, melanin content, and epidermis thickness from spectral diffuse reflectance. Appl Opt 49(10):1707-1719.
Bjorgan A, Milanic M, Randeberg LL (2014) Estimation of skin optical parameters for real-time hyperspectral imaging applications. J Biomed Opt 19(6):066003.
Calin MA, Parasca SV, Savastru R, Manea D (2014) Hyperspectral imaging in the medical field: present and future. Appl Spectrosc Rev 49(6):435-447.
Akarcay HG et al. (2014) Optical capsule for in vivo gastrointestinal imaging: Monte Carlo simulation. Biomed Opt Express 5(2):412-426.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-040 DOT (Diffuse Optical Tomography) inverts transmission-geometry diffuse-reflectance through bulk tissue (cm-scale, multi-source-multi-detector, full tomographic acquisition); sensing_mechanism = diffuse_optical_tomography; solution_space = 3D tissue optical map. L1-511 PillCam-SPECTRA inverts capsule-reflectance geometry (mm-scale, single-side source-detector pair, single-frame acquisition); sensing_mechanism = diffuse_reflectance_capsule; solution_space = 2D mucosal chromophore concentration map. Different geometric primitive (L.diffuse_reflectance vs L.diffuse_tomography), different solution dimensionality (2D mucosal patch vs 3D bulk), different scale (mm vs cm). Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, solution_space, primitives) ~ 0.40 — Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30). Note: this Principle serves as the analytical core that the PWDR wrapper L1-512 references via its physics_core_principle field — establishing the v2 pattern.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Capsule endoscopy is established for small-bowel obstructed bleeding evaluation (Crohn's, NSAID enteropathy, occult bleeding). Multispectral / hyperspectral PillCam variants (PillCam-SPECTRA prototype) extend diagnostic value: chromophore mapping of mucosal HbO2, Hb, water — basis for enhanced detection of bleeding sites, polyps, vascular anomalies, dysplasia. Director's GI_Multi_Task active research project aligns directly. Currently pre-clinical to early clinical; commercial systems include Medtronic PillCam SB3 / Crohn's, Olympus Endocapsule 10, Intromedic MiroCam — multispectral / hyperspectral upgrades pending.
